Abbie Yunita.bio: I hold a BA in Geography and Environmental Science; International Studies (1st Class Honours) from Monash University in Melbourne; an MA in Development Studies ( Summa Cum Laude ) from the Graduate Institute of International Development Studies in Geneva; and a PhD in Sustainability Studies from Utrecht University.…

Abe Hendriks.bio: Dr. Abe Hendriks is a researcher and lecturer at the Urban Futures Studio and the Innovation Studies section at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development.

Andries Hof.bio: Welcome to my profile page. I'm Andries Hof, since 2015 guest researcher at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development of Utrecht University.

Anna van der Kaaden.bio: Teaching: Systems Thinking, Climate and Society, Ecosystem Change in the Anthropocene (University College Utrecht)

Anne Rainville.bio: Research focus: Demand-side mechanisms for innovation and sustainability - innovation procurement, circular economy, and standardization

Annisa Triyanti.bio: Research focus: flood risk reduction, permeable dams, aquaculture systems, integrated coastal management, climate adaptation pathways at deltas

Arie Staal.bio: I am Assistant Professor of Ecosystem Resilience at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development. I study how feedbacks within ecosystems, and between ecosystems and the climate, amplify or dampen the effects of global changes.

Arturo Castillo Castillo.bio: Arturo investigates the conditions for transitioning to a less dissipative, less toxic, more resource-efficient society. He creates multi-disciplinary approaches to tackle wicked problems such as plastic pollution and dependence on critical raw materials.

Benjamin Delory.bio: I am a functional, chemical, and community ecologist fascinated by plant-plant and plant-soil interactions.

Blanca Corona Bellostas.bio: Blanca is an associate professor at the Energy and Resources group of the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development. Her main research interest and expertise are related with the sustainability and circularity assessments of products and services.

Bonno Pel.bio: Dr. Bonno Pel is assistant professor in critical innovation studies.

Brian Dermody.bio: I hold an interdisciplinary Assistant Professor position at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development , Utrecht University. I am positioned within the Environmental Sciences Department and work to build interdisciplinary collaboration among the 5 departments within the Copernicus Institute

Britta Ricker.bio: Additionally, I am a voting representative for the Netherlands to the International Cartographic Association where I am the Chair of the Commission of Cartography and Sustainable Development https://carto-sd.icaci.org/

Carole-Anne Sénit.bio: Carole-Anne SÃ©nit is Assistant Professor of Inclusive Sustainability Governance. She is a political scientist with a career spanning both research on and practice in the civil society sector.

Christina Bidmon.bio: Christina explores how societal change processes such as the shift to a sustainable, circular economy or the digital transformation affect organizationsâ innovation strategies and, in turn, how organizationsâ innovation efforts affect institutional arrangements. She is also passionate about the question of how…

Detlef van Vuuren.bio: Detlef van Vuuren focuses on global sustainability issues. With his team, he develops models with which future climate and environmental changes can be explored by means of scenarios.

Diana Vela Almeida.bio: Currently, her research topics can be grouped in two core areas. The first critically questions the green transition as a new colonial frontier of extraction in the Global South and looks at the unrecognized reproductive labor that powers the green energy transition.

Dries Hegger.bio: Research focus: citizen engagement in regional water- and climate governance, improved science-policy interactions through knowledge co-creation

Elena Fumagalli.bio: Elena M. Fumagalli is an Associate Professor of Energy Economics at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development, Section Energy & Resources.

Ernst Worrell.bio: Relations between energy and material use and the environment, and possibilities to limit these by means of efficiency improvement.

expertise: Land Use and Land Use Change
Floortje van der Hilst.bio: Floor van der Hilst is full professor Sustainable Land use and she is the chair of the section Energy & Resources of the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development. Her research focusses on the challenge of how we can achieve sustainable land use and management in the face of intensifying and often competing…

Flor Avelino.bio: Flor Avelino has been working as a researcher and lecturer in sustainability transitions and transformative social innovation since 2005. With a background in political science, she specialises in power theories and has a particular interest in understanding how people, organisations and networks are (dis)empowered to…

Frank Biermann.bio: Frank Biermann is a research professor of Global Sustainability Governance with the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development at Utrecht University, The Netherlands. He is an internationally leading scholar of global institutions and organizations in the sustainability domain.

Frank van Laerhoven.bio: I study environmental governance, particularly the governance of ecosystems. My research agenda includes an interest in common pool resources (CPRs), socio-ecological systems, decentralization reforms, local democracy and participation, and the solving of collective action dilemmas.

Frank van Rijnsoever.bio: I'm curious about how innovation and entrepreneurship work exactly. Further, I believe that science has the task of making its knowledge accessible to society.

G Koren.bio: My research areas are the carbon and water cycle, with a focus on the exchange of CO 2 between the atmosphere and biosphere in tropical regions. I want to contribute to a better understanding of the impact of environmental conditions on this exchange, for example the response of the Amazon forest to severe droughts (…

Gabriela de Pinho.bio: I work as an Assistant professor at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development , Utrecht University. I have a background in Nutritional Sciences and Epidemiology and in 2019 I obtained my PhD with the thesis entitled Contextual correlates of dietary behaviours in adults across Europe .

expertise: Economic Geography of Science and Innovation
Gaston Heimeriks.bio: "How can we produce transformative science that society needs?"

Gert Jan Kramer.bio: Since May 2016 Gert Jan Kramer is Professor of Sustainable Energy Supply Systems at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development at Utrecht University. From 1 January 2018 onwards Gert Jan is head of the Energy & Resources group within this institute.

Giovanna Capponi.bio: Giovanna Capponi's research deals with the incentives, dynamics, and mobility of people and ideas that drive knowledge and innovation. She studies intellectual property rights and their role in shaping firms' innovation strategies, including in the context of the circular economy; she develops empirical methods to…

Giuseppe Feola.bio: I am Associate Professor of Social Change for Sustainability in the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development at Utrecht University, the Netherlands.

Hamed Aslannejad.bio: Currently, Hamed is an Assistant Professor at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development, where he is a key player in hydrogen production, storage, and use. He leads several projects focused on Green Hydrogen production and use.

Harriet Bulkeley.bio: Harriet Bulkeley holds joint appointments as Professor in the Department of Geography, Durham University, and at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development, Utrecht University. Her research focuses on environmental governance and the politics of climate change, energy and sustainable cities.

Heleen Mees.bio: Heleen Mees is an Associate Professor of local sustainability governance. She is the research coordinator and member of the Management Team of the Environmental Governance group.

Hens Runhaar.bio: Research focus: Policy and governance for reconciling food production, biodiversity recovery, environmental sustainability, and health

Hugo de Boer.bio: Hugo de Boer is Professor of Nature-based Climate Adaptation in Deltas at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development and acts as Geosciences scientific lead for the Delta Climate Center in Vlissingen. He studies the dynamic responses of plants and ecosystems to global change drivers and coordinates the MSc…

Ilse van Liempt.bio: Ilse van Liempt is Professor of Geographies of Migration and Urban Inequalities and Director of Resarch in the Human Geography and Spatial Planning Department. She is also one of the research leaders of the UU wide Focus Area Migration and Societal Change .

Iris Wanzenböck.bio: Iris is an Associate Professor at the Innovation Studies Group, Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development at Utrecht University. She has a broad interest in the geography and governance of innovation.

Iryna Susha.bio: Iryna Susha is an Assistant Professor at the Innovation Studies group of the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development, Faculty of Geosciences. Her research focuses on how organizations from different sectors can collaborate and leverage data to address societal challenges of the 21st century and contribute to…

James Patterson.bio: I am an interdisciplinary social scientist working at the intersection of sustainability, public policy, and comparative politics. My research examines the policy, politics, and governance of complex public good problems â especially climate change â at national and subnational scales.

Jana Cox.bio: My name is Jana Cox and I worked on both my PhD and postdoc (in the physical geography department) with the NKWK Rivers2Morrow ( https://ncr-web.org/projects/rivers2morrow/ ) at Utrecht University project studying the response of the lower Rhine and Maas rivers to climate change and sea level rise. Following this, I…

Janpieter van der Pol.bio: My research focusses on the analysis of innovation ecosystems with an overarching topic on technological sovereignty. I'm interested in understanding how innovation ecosystems work and how they can be adapted to adress sovereignty issues for critical technologies.

Jarno Hoekman.bio: Jarno Hoekman is an associate professor in science and innovation studies. His research focuses on the dynamics and governance of science in the context of innovation, regulation and societal challenges, with a particular focus on sustainability and health.

Jasper Griffioen.bio: Professor Jasper Griffioen has a part-time chair Water Quality Management at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development (section Environmental Sciences) at the Faculty of Geosciences of Utrecht University. He is also an expert researcher at TNO Geological Survey of the Netherlands.

Jeroen Oomen.bio: Jeroen Oomen is assistant professor at the Urban Futures Studio, where he focuses on the social, cultural, and scientific practices that create societies' conceptions of the future. Coming from an interdisciplinary background spanning philosophy, political science, sociology, and the history of science and technology,…

Jerry van Dijk.bio: Welcome to my personal profile page. I’m Associate Professor at the Environmental Sciences section of the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development of Utrecht University, The Netherlands.

Jesse Hoffman.bio: Research focus: social impact of energy transition, political science, futuring, energy poverty, civic participation

Jing Hu.bio: I am an interdisciplinary researcher specialising in the modelling of the transition of energy systems towards net-zero emissions under changing socioeconomic and hydroclimatic conditions, considering multiple dimensions of the sustainable development goals. My research line spans 3 interconnected areas.

Joeri Wesseling.bio: Joeri Wesseling is an assistant professor in innovation studies. His work focuses on sustainability transitions in the automotive, energy and energy-intensive processing industries.

Joost Vervoort.bio: Joost Vervoort is Associate Professor of Transformative Imagination in the Environmental Governance Group at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development.

Josephine Chambers.bio: A political ecologist by training, her doctoral research at the University of Cambridge dissected the politics of 'win-win' frames in nature conservation , and problematized common approaches to failure and learning . Her subsequent analysis of diverse initiatives co-producing research and action for sustainability…

expertise: Parliamentary and Alternative Forms of Democracy

Julian Kirchherr.bio: Research focus : Circular economy, Sustainability transitions

Kaj-Ivar van der Wijst.bio: Kaj-Ivar van der Wijst is an assistant professor in Global Modelling at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development. He has contributed to the IPCC Working Group III (Mitigation of Climate Change) and the Synthesis Report of the 6th Assessment Report.

Karlijn van den Broek.bio: People are both the cause of and the solution to our most pressing sustainability challenges. I study how individuals and groups mentally represent complex environmental problems, and how these representations shape the decisions that drive â or hinder â sustainable change.

Katerina Kermeli.bio: Katerina Kermeli is a researcher in the Energy & Resources Group of the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development working since 2010 on a variety of projects related to industrial energy efficiency and decarbonisation.

Koen Beumer.bio: Dr. Koen Beumer is associate professor in Science, Technology, and Society (STS).

Koen Frenken.bio: Research focus: radical innovation, economic geography, platform economy, innovation policy

Kristina Bogner.bio: My name is Kristina and I am an interdisciplinary researcher and teacher enthusiastic about engaging in sustainability transitions and transformations . My mission is to support transformative social innovation, peoplesâ development, and impactful and responsible thinking and behavior .

Li Shen.bio: My research focuses on the sustainability assessment of (novel) technology developed in the context of the Circular Economy and the Bio-based Economy , the two focus areas that will provide important pathways towards a sustainable material production and consumption in the future.

Lukas Weimann.bio: In his spare time, Lukas follows online courses in the fields of business and operations management to satisfy his broad spectrum of interests.

Maarten Hajer.bio: Maarten A. Hajer (1962) is distinguished professor Urban Futures and Futuring and Director of the Urban Futures Studio .

Marco Hekkert.bio: Research focus: sustainable innovations and transitioning towards a sustainable society

My research focuses on urban water management, nature-based solutions, and planning support systems for water-sensitive urban design, with the aim of contributing to sustainable…

Martin Wassen.bio: I am driven by curiosity about how ecosystems work and interact with the environment. I am an ecologist working on terrestrial and wetland ecosystems and their functioning in a changing world (land use change, water management, global change).

Maryse Chappin.bio: My research mainly focuses on gaining a better understanding of (the process of) sustainable innovation. I focus on collaboration and networks as well as on the impact of the institutional environment on (sustainable) innovation.

Matteo Gazzani.bio: Before joining the Copernicus Institute, Dr. Matteo Gazzani worked as senior scientist (2016-2017) and as postdoctoral fellow (2013-2016) in the Institute of Process Engineering at ETH Zurich.

Matthijs Janssen.bio: Trained as an innovation scientist, Matthijs investigates innovation policy and strategy. As tenured assistant professor at the Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development, Matthijs mainly focuses at industrial policy, transitions, mission-oriented innovation policy, and novel forms of smart specialization.

Max Rietkerk.bio: Full professor Spatial Ecology and Global Change, Head Environmental Sciences at Copernicus Institute of Sustainable Development at Utrecht University, The Netherlands.

In this role, I use agent-based modeling to understand the…

Nico Brinkel.bio: Nico Brinkel conducts research on electric vehicle charging. His research focus is on optimization of charging processes and analyses on the grid impact of electric vehicle charging.

Niko Wojtynia.bio: Niko Wojtynia researches the sustainability transition of food and agricultural systems and teaches in the Global Sustainability Science program. His current research concerns the transformation of farming systems in Dutch regions.

Paul Schot.bio: Associate professor of Environmental Sciences and Ecohydrology

Peter Driessen.bio: Peter Driessen is full professor of environmental governance. He holds degrees in urban and regional planning and in political sciences.

Peter Mulder.bio: I am an economist by training, and I work part-time as Associate Professor at the Copernicus Institute for Sustainable Development of Utrecht University, next to my job as Senior Researcher at TNO Energy Transition Studies .

In the Future Food Hub, I work on agricultural…

Takeshi Kuramochi.bio: Takeshi holds M.Sc. (environmental sciences) and Ph.D.

Tessa de Geus.bio: Iâm an action researcher working at the intersection of academia and practice with a focus on social innovation and transformative change in Europe. I explore how people and initiatives navigate power in transitions: moving between being rooted in radical ideals and engaging with established systems and institutions.

Her research interests lie in the area of circular and sustainable business models.
